behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (manifest validator -> plugin importer/executor), not just inside one function. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess/OS primitive is required; local plugin directory wiring is enough. REJECTION-GATE: System considered then rejected because behavior text is only about a plugin in a configured directory and rejection-before-execution, not subprocess/real HTTP/OS-event behavior. Demotion below Agentic is valid: deterministic plumbing target is "manifest is missing a required field is rejected"; model-output dependence is excluded because behavior is schema validation/execution gating, with no AI/ML call.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one plugin whose manifest omits a required key and whose entry module has a visible side effect (for example writing a sentinel file or incrementing a shared counter on import). Run the real loader over that directory and assert: (1) validation error is reported for that plugin, (2) sentinel side effect never happens, proving no plugin code executed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when dependency resolver and lifecycle invoker interact; unit-testing only topo-sort or only hook runner can miss it. REAL-INFRA: can be caught in-process with local filesystem plugins; no subprocess/network needed. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is only hook ordering by declared dependency, not real OS/network/subprocess semantics. Demotion below Agentic is valid: deterministic plumbing target is dependency-based ordering of hooks; no behavior text ties correctness to model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Build two real test plugins in a temp directory: A depends on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory log (or file log). Execute loader and assert log order is `["B","A"]` and both loaded successfully.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is cycle detection + load gating; need interaction between graph analysis and loader execution decisions. A pure unit cycle test would miss accidental loading despite detected cycle. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network/OS primitive required. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior requires only local dependency graph handling and load suppression. Demotion below Agentic is valid: deterministic plumbing target is cycle report and skip-load behavior; no AI/ML output quality or variability is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A, with entry modules that emit unmistakable side effects if loaded. Run loader and assert: (1) cycle error mentions A<->B, (2) neither onLoad runs, and (3) no entry-module side effects occurred for either plugin.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering across importer execution and lifecycle dispatch; unit tests of either part alone can miss sequencing bugs. REAL-INFRA: requires real in-process module import semantics but not subprocess/real network/OS-event behavior. REJECTION-GATE: System considered because runtime import behavior is involved, but rejected since behavior text only requires loader sequencing around module import/execute, which is testable in-process without external infra. Demotion below Agentic is valid: deterministic plumbing target is call sequence (import/execute then onLoad); behavior excludes AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin entry module that sets a global/shared flag during module execution, and an onLoad that asserts the flag is already set (or records observed state). Run loader and assert onLoad sees post-import state; fail if onLoad runs before module side effect.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in cross-plugin orchestration (error isolation + dependency-aware continuation). Unit tests of try/catch alone cannot verify dependency-filtered continuation. REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/network/OS primitive needed; local multi-plugin wiring is sufficient. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior is loader control-flow under hook exceptions, not external infrastructure behavior. Demotion below Agentic is valid: deterministic plumbing target is continuation policy after thrown error; no model output content or non-deterministic AI decision is part of correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins: `core` throws in onLoad; `independent` has no dependency on `core`; `dependent` depends on `core`. Execute loader and assert: error for `core` is captured, `independent` still loads, and `dependent` is skipped/failed due to dependency. Verify final status map reflects this split outcome.
